title: Simple Closet Organizer
summary: A wall-mounted plywood shelving box built from wide ripped strips, screwed straight into the closet studs — six shelves between two full-height sides, no separate cabinet to assemble.
description:
This is a single fixed shelving box, not a modular tower system — rip 3/4" plywood or particle board into wide strips (referred to throughout as "1x16", but rip to match your own closet's depth) and build a plain box: two full-height sides with six shelves between them, either pocket-holed from inside the shelves or predrilled and screwed through the sides. Glue every joint and check for square before it goes anywhere near the wall.

A 1/4" plywood back nailed and glued across the whole assembly is what keeps the box from racking once it's loaded — nail into every shelf edge as you go, not just the two sides, for real rigidity. A 1x3 footer glued and nailed to the base, set slightly inset from the front, keeps the bottom edge off the closet floor.

**This shelf must land on a stud.** Position the finished unit in the closet and screw through the back into a wall stud with L brackets; if a stud doesn't fall where you need it, use drywall anchors rated for the load instead. Leftover 1x16 scraps make extra shelves elsewhere in the closet — screw a scrap 1x3 cleat to the wall on each side at the height you want and rest a shelf on top, adding a closet rod below if needed.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood or MDF, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

cutList.material: 3/4" plywood or MDF, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet
3/4" plywood or MDF, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 82-1/2" and six shelves at 25-1/2" from the 3/4" plywood or MDF, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet. Cut one back at 82-1/2" from the 1/4" plywood or MDF, 4x8 sheet. Cut one footer at 25-1/2" from the 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Mark the sides for shelf positions. Drill pocket holes in the shelves and screw to the sides, or predrill through the sides into the shelves. Glue every joint and adjust for square.
materials: 3/4" plywood or MDF, ripped to 15-1/2" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Nail and glue the back panel to the box, nailing into every shelf edge, not just the sides, for real rigidity.

steps.title: Add the footer
steps.body: Glue and nail the 1x3 footer to the base, inset slightly from the front edge.

steps.title: Mount in the closet
steps.body: Position the unit in the closet and screw through the back into a wall stud with L brackets. If no stud lands in the right spot, use drywall anchors rated for the load instead.

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple wall-mounted closet organizer with six shelves
